task_name: European Basic Laparoscopic Urological Skills (E-BLUS) Examination - Multi-Task Presentation
note: This is a research conference presentation about E-BLUS examination results, not a single trainee performance video. It contains multiple task demonstrations (Peg Transfer, Pattern Cut, Endoloop, Extracorporeal Suture, Intracorporeal Suture) used as examples within a scientific presentation.
reason_not_scored: Video is a structured academic presentation showing multiple task examples and normative data, not a single trainee attempting to complete a task. Individual task segments are demonstration clips embedded within lecture slides, making completion time and penalty assessment inappropriate without isolated performance context.

tasks_demonstrated.frames_shown: frames at 0:01, 0:27 - expert vs participant comparison
demonstrated_concepts: Expert performance showing rapid instrument control and bimanual coordination
Participant performance showing slower peg manipulation
Normative data: median time ~120 seconds for examined cohort (histogram shows peak 100-150 sec range)
Gauze grasping and stabilization between clamps
Completion marked as 'Finished' within target time (2:49)
Task shows clean cutting approach with sustained tension

tasks_demonstrated.frames_shown: frames at 0:08, 1:24, 2:49 - suture placement and knot tying
demonstrated_concepts: Suture needle passage through marked points
Extracorporeal knot formation with knot pusher
Video shows completion within target time (2:49) but notes speed increase via fast-forward

tasks_demonstrated.frames_shown: frames at 0:23, 2:25, 4:11, 3:38 - knot tying on kidney model
demonstrated_concepts: Blue and red suture visible on yellow kidney block training model
Intracorporeal knot formation with hand switching
Multiple throw sequence shown with time progression
One example shows 'target time exceeded' at 4:11 (exceeds 600s max)
Another example completed at 3:38 within time
Instructional text visible: 'stitch not through the black dot' — indicating marking precision requirement

teaching_content.presentation_structure: Results of multi-center European Basic Laparoscopic Urological Skills examination
demonstrated_techniques: Proper instrument grasp and control for peg transfer
Extracorporeal and intracorporeal knot tying with hand alternation
Normative performance benchmarks across novice/intermediate/expert levels
verbal_cues: Video includes slide text: 'Despite the growing popularity of robot-assisted laparoscopy in urology, conventional laparoscopic surgery is still the established technique'
Emphasis on simulator-based skills training acceptance
Discussion slide: 'Majority of participants do not reach the criteria. Quality is scored relatively well, but time is low → more skillslab training could benefit these participants'
common_errors_addressed: Low completion time relative to quality (indicating need for speed optimization)
Failure to reach precision marks (black dot marking visible in suture tasks)
Time management during complex tasks (several examples show timeout exceeded)
teaching_content.skill_level_explanation: Presentation shows both expert demonstration clips (fast, efficient peg transfer) and novice/intermediate participant clips (slower times, marginal marking accuracy). Explicitly contrasts expert performance with typical participant cohort performance.
research_findings_highlighted: Normative 'Time task 3' histogram shows peak at 100-150 seconds for cohort
Generalized examinee-centered method for setting norms across experience levels
Log-scale plot showing relationship between experience (number of laparoscopic procedures) and time/quality performance
Study conclusion: Low training volume and hours explains low overall scores; quality acceptable but speed needs improvement

cannot_score_reasons: No isolated single-task performance — video is a research presentation with embedded demonstration clips
Multiple task examples spliced together with educational content interspersed
Timestamps shown are illustrative of presentation flow, not continuous task execution
Some clips show 'fast forward (4x)' notation, indicating non-real-time playback
Completion times visible (e.g., 2:49, 4:11) but lack context for penalty assessment (penalties not visible, no dropped objects or placement errors documented)
Academic/normative context (comparing participant to expert and showing research data) precludes standard FLS rubric application
confidence_rationale: Video classification as instructional is high-confidence based on title slide 'Results of the European Basic Laparoscopic Urological Skills Examination', research author list, background/aim/discussion slides, and explicit reference to norms and cohort data. The embedded task demonstrations are clearly illustrative examples, not performance assessments.
